commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: And she will never fall, who puts before her eyes modesty, and her shawl; nor will she invite another to fall into sin by uncovering her face. For this is the wish of the Word, since it is becoming for her to pray veiled.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
(although that even they have the right of prophesying, he has already shown.
For they who allow to virgins immunity from head-covering, appear to rest on this; that the apostle has not defined "virgins "by name, but "women".
dishonoureth her own head."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: What is the meaning of the expression “every woman” except women of every age, every rank and every circumstance? No one is excepted.

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: And he says: "Every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered, dishonoureth her head"

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
